free_text: Excitation functions and yields of relevance to the
production of Ga-67 by proton bombardment of Zn-nat
and Ge-nat up to 100 MeV
The computer code 'ALICE'.
The total uncertainties in the cross
sections and production rates were obtained by
summing all the contributing uncertainties in
quadrature and are expressed with a 68% confidence
level.
The uncertainties in beam-loss corrections
The uncertainties in beam-current.
The uncertainties in the detector efficiency.
The uncertainties in counting geometry.
The uncertainties in decay corrections.
The uncertainties in the foil thickness.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents. Subents 002, 003 were
split (Subents 004-009 added). MONITOR/REF added.
In the two low-energy Zn stacks 100 um thick high-
purity Zn foils(99.99%) were employed, while for the
high-energy stack discs with a thickness of 300 um
were prepared by cold uniaxial pressing of high-purity
Zn metal powder(99.9%) at a pressure of 523 mpA.
/By authors/ Cross-sections of Ga-68 could not be
obtained above 45.1 MeV due to the fact that the
characteristic gamma-ray used for its determination
(1077.4 keV, 2.93%) could not be extracted from the
background in the gamma-ray spectra of these particular
foils.
Tbl.1 from Appl.Rad.Isol.,42(1991)353
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
DATA-ERR->ERR-T. Subent was split (see Subent 004-006).
The Ge stacks discs of polycrystalline metal(99.99%,),
cut to 250 um thickness, were used throughout. Each
target foil, except those in the lower-energy (less
20 MeV) part of the two 40 MeV stacks, was followed by
a 250 um high-purity Al monitor foil (99.999%) .
Tbl.2 from Appl.Rad.Isol.,42(1991)353
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
DATA-ERR->ERR-T. Subent was split (see Subent 007-011).
